subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: A clear research question sets a strong foundation for the exploration.
Paper follows a recognisable structure: introduction, data description, calculations, conclusion.
Tables and headings provide adequate organization of material.
weaknesses: Lengthy keystroke instructions and screenshots interrupt the statistical narrative.
Redundant explanations (e.g., repeated chi-squared definitions) reduce conciseness.
Inconsistent citation formatting and unnecessary administrative details disrupt flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work is organised with an introduction, body and conclusion and shows logical development, but digressions on calculator keystrokes, duplicate explanations and heavy screenshots stall the reader. These factors align with band 2 for presentation: adequate structure and coherence but limited conciseness.

strengths: Uses key statistical symbols (ÏÂ², p-value, Hâ) and some correct notation.
Includes multiple data representations: tables of percentages, counts and a bar chart.
Explanations are largely understandable and guide the reader through the analysis.
weaknesses: Chi-squared formula is mis-typed (no square on the numerator).
No table of expected frequencies is presented to justify calculations.
Inconsistent decimal formatting and missing axis labels reduce clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are employed but not consistently accurate; the student uses some appropriate representations but stops short of effective multiplicity; overall communication is mostly clear with lapses. These characteristics correspond to band 2 in mathematical communication.

strengths: Choosing a culturally sensitive topic and sourcing external survey data shows independent thinking.
A personal narrative about growing up in Sri Lanka drives the choice of research question.
The iterative narrowing of focus (e.g., on lesbian/gay only) demonstrates personal approach.
weaknesses: Methodological choices remain conventional with no original modeling.
Creativity is shown in topic selection but not in extending the mathematics.
Engagement is clear but does not âhighly enhanceâ mathematical insight.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but creativity and initiative in mathematical aspects remain limited. This places the work solidly in band 2 for personal engagement.

strengths: The student notes unexpected results and tool limitations, indicating some reflection.
A brief discussion of personal growth and statistical understanding is present.
weaknesses: Reflection is largely descriptive and lacks sustained depth.
Critical analysis of test assumptions and data reliability is missing.
Connections to mathematical understanding are superficial and general.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student makes some reflective comments on results and personal learning, the analysis is basic and the connection to deeper understanding is limited. This aligns with band 1 in reflection.

strengths: Application of the ÏÂ² test is directly relevant and at the correct SL level.
Hypotheses are stated and observed counts are converted from percentages.
Mathematics used is appropriate to the research question context.
weaknesses: Observed counts retain decimals instead of whole numbers; rounding issues.
Expected frequencies and residuals are omitted, reducing rigour.
Misinterpretation of a tiny p-value contradicts correct rejection of Hâ.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant SL-level mathematics but with partial correctness (truncation errors, missing expected counts) and some misunderstanding in interpretation. These factors correspond to band 2 in use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a culturally sensitive topic and sourcing an external survey shows **significant independent thinking**; the student chooses and justifies a statistical technique without merely following a template. Shortcomings in depth prevent placement in the highest band.

The student shows some understanding of hypotheses, p-values and significance but misunderstands the meaning of a tiny p-value when stating that results show âno strong linkâ. This demonstrates **some but not thorough understanding**, appropriate for band 2.

Observed counts are computed with decimals then truncated; expected frequencies and residuals are omitted, and the conclusion contradicts earlier interpretation. Hence mathematics is **partially correct**, placing the work in band 2.

Large sections explain keyboard shortcuts, include duplicate tables or restate definitions already given. These additions are **irrelevant to answering the research question** and reduce conciseness. Although the student does attempt to stay on topic overall, the amount of superfluous text keeps the strand in band 1.

Key statistical terms (ÏÂ², p-value, Hâ) appear and some notation is correct; however, the chi-squared formula is mis-typed (no square on the numerator) and decimals are inconsistently shown. **Language is generally understandable but not consistently accurate**, fitting band 2.

Explanations are mostly clear, but inconsistency arises when the conclusion paragraph contradicts earlier statements about rejecting Hâ. Minor notation slips also reduce consistency. Hence, communication is **mostly clear with some lapses**, aligning with band 2.

The chi-squared test is directly relevant and at the correct AI-SL level, applied to genuine categorical data. Although the method is not extended, this constitutes **relevant mathematics used at course level**, matching band 3.

The student offers tables of percentages, tables of counts and one bar chart, which together give **some appropriate representations** of the data. There is no expected-values table, residual plot or other alternative form, so the work stops short of âeffectiveâ or âsophisticatedâ use; band 2 is warranted.

The student comments on unexpected results and tool limitations, which constitutes **limited reflection**. Discussion is brief and largely descriptive, lacking sustained depth; consequently band 1 is appropriate.

Including screenshots, arrows and informal commentary shows **some creativity**, but methodological choices remain conventional and no original mathematical model is produced. Evidence is therefore limited to band 1.

The investigation follows a recognisable pathâintroduction, data description, calculations, and conclusionâso the reader can follow the storyline. However, **logical development is uneven**: long digressions on calculator keystrokes interrupt the statistical argument and some steps (e.g., conversion of percentages to counts) are repeated. These weaknesses prevent the work from reaching the clear, well-sequenced standard of band 3, but it is still more coherent than a minimally organised piece.

Critical analysis is superficial: the student notes calculator rounding issues and sample size but does not probe data reliability, expected counts, or test assumptions. This **basic critique** meets band 1 only.

Connections to personal understanding are made (âthis project deepened my understanding of statistical analysisâ), yet explanations remain general. The strand shows a **limited link to mathematical learning**, meriting band 1.

The paper contains an introduction, body and brief conclusion; tables and headings give **adequate structure**. Nevertheless, material such as device screenshots and lengthy how-to instructions are placed mid-analysis, causing the flow to stall. Because organisation is serviceable yet clearly not âwell-organised with seamless flowâ, band 2 is most appropriate.

A personal narrative about growing up in Sri Lanka and iterative decisions (e.g., narrowing to lesbian/gay only) reveal a **clear personal approach that drives the exploration**. While genuine, it does not âhighly enhance understandingâ, so band 2 fits best.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and focused, providing a strong foundation for the exploration.
The note âPage number:18â is unnecessary in the main text and interrupts flow; remove such administrative details.
The personal narrative about growing up in Sri Lanka effectively demonstrates a clear personal engagement and motivation.
The full screenshot of YouTube comments is visually heavy and disrupts the narrative; consider summarizing key quotes instead.
The chi-squared test definition is repeated in multiple paragraphs, reducing conciseness; consolidate this explanation once.
The screenshot of the sampling section interrupts the methodological narrative; move this to an appendix or describe it in text.
The detailed description of sampling consumes space without adding analytical insight; summarise key points more concisely.
No table of expected frequencies is presented before applying the chi-squared formula; include one to justify your calculations.
The passage reiterating why the chi-squared test is used duplicates earlier text and could be condensed.
Observed counts in Table 2 retain decimals, yet they must be whole numbers; round or adjust appropriately before analysis.
The conversion formula is applied but results are truncated; include rationale for rounding and ensure totals remain consistent.
The chi-squared formula is misâtyped as $$\chi^2=\frac{\Sigma(OâE)}{E}$$; correct it to $$\chi^2=\sum\frac{(OâE)^2}{E}$$ for accuracy.
Graph 1 lacks axis labels and a legend, making interpretation difficult; add clear labels and units to improve clarity.
The list of TI-Nspire keystrokes in the analysis section interrupts the statistical flow; move detailed instructions to an appendix.
The extensive keystroke instructions for creating a matrix add irrelevant detail; summarise the process briefly.
Instructions on saving and recalling the matrix are repeated in multiple sections; consolidate to a single note or appendix.
The calculator interface screenshot is too large and disrupts the analysis; consider reducing its size or moving it to supplementary materials.
The bullet explaining matrix storage appears twice; remove duplicate list to improve conciseness.
Expected frequencies are referenced but not shown; include the computations to demonstrate rigour in your chi-square test.
The conclusion paragraph stating âno strong linkâ contradicts the rejection of $$H_0$$ based on $$p<0.05$$; reconcile these statements for consistency.
Interpretation of the p-value is clear, but the text lacks deeper critical analysis of assumptions (e.g., sample representativeness).
The reflective section articulates personal growth and challenges, demonstrating meaningful engagement with the research process.
References in the bibliography follow inconsistent formats; standardize citation style (e.g., APA) throughout.
